system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.5;

maxDeltaT       0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi)        Gauss upwind;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
attempt to read beyond EOF

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/pitzDaily/3/system/fvSchemes/divSchemes/div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) at line 30.

    From function virtual Foam::Istream& Foam::ITstream::read(Foam::token&)
    in file db/IOstreams/Tstreams/ITstream.C at line 79.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is the same as before:

```
attempt to read beyond EOF
file: .../system/fvSchemes/divSchemes/div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) at line 30.
```

This means the solver cannot parse the divergence scheme entry `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))`. Despite Attempt 3 adding `div(phi)` and Attempt 2 suggesting quoting, the fundamental parsing issue persists.

Let me carefully examine the current `fvSchemes`'s `divSchemes` section:

```
divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi)        Gauss upwind;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
}
```

The problem is that the entry `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` has **three opening parentheses** and **three closing parentheses**. When the parser sees `div(` followed by `(nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))`, it tries to extract the argument for the divergence scheme. But `upwind` consumes only one token (the word `upwind`), leaving `)))` unparsed, which causes the "read beyond EOF" error.

## Root Cause

In OpenFOAM's divergence scheme parsing, the format is:
```
div(<fieldName>) <schemeName> [<schemeOptions>];
```

The `<fieldName>` is parsed as a single token. For `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))`, the field name itself contains nested parentheses, making it impossible to parse unambiguously with `upwind` as a separate token.

## Solution

The divergence scheme entry for the stress term needs to be written so that the field name is clearly delimited. There are two approaches:

### Approach 1: Remove the entry entirely and rely on `default`

Since the `default` divergence scheme is set to `none`, removing the problematic entry would cause a different error. Instead, **change the divergence scheme for the stress term to use `default`**:

```
div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) default;
```

This tells OpenFOAM to use whatever the fallback/default scheme is (which is `Gauss linear corrected` based on the `laplacianSchemes` section).

### Approach 2: Use a simpler explicit form

If the above doesn't work, the safest approach is to **replace the complex divergence scheme entry with a simple one**. Since the user requirement specifies a RAS simulation with k-epsilon turbulence, the simplest working approach is:

Remove `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` from `divSchemes` entirely, and add instead:

```
div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
```

But written with **minimal parentheses** so the parser can handle it. Actually, looking at the pitzDaily tutorial more carefully for OpenFOAM 10, the correct format should be:

```
div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
```

The key insight from the previous attempts is that `Gauss linearUpwind` for `div(phi,U)` works fine, but the stress divergence scheme entry keeps failing. The issue is specifically with how `upwind` parses when the field name has embedded parentheses.

**Try changing `div(phi,U)` from `Gauss linearUpwind` to `Gauss upwind`** as well, to make all divergence entries use the simple `Gauss <keyword>` format where `<keyword>` is a single word.

## Summary

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `system/fvSchemes` | Change `div(phi,U)` from `Gauss linearUpwind` to `Gauss upwind` |
| `system/fvSchemes` | Change `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` from `Gauss upwind` to `Gauss upwind` (same format, but ensure no extra whitespace issues) |

The core fix is ensuring all divergence scheme entries use the simple `Gauss <single_word>` format. If the stress entry continues to fail, **remove it entirely** from `divSchemes` and add a `laplacianSchemes` entry for it:

```
laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
}
```

But the primary action is switching `div(phi,U)` to `Gauss upwind` to rule out any parsing ambiguity.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Change div(phi,U) from Gauss linearUpwind to Gauss upwind; Change div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) from Gauss upwind to Gauss upwind -- actually remove this entry entirely from divSchemes since it causes parsing issues; Add laplacianSchemes entry for div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) using Gauss upwind if needed, but first try removing it from divSchemes'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
